Mtn To Strike First Amended Complt; Req For Judicial Ntc; Pos
Off Calendar Dec-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MARITIME NATIONAL PARK ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Lauren S. Nishimura of Bingham McCutchen LLP (393.2332) for defendant San Francisco Maritime National Park Association; Megan Jeanne of The Corporate Law Group (650.227.8000) for defendant TWG, LLC; Dominick R. Libonati of Mallette & Libonati (650.347.5400) appearing telephonically for plaintiff Andy Kilday Event Production Services. Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted without leave to amend. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. One order on demurrer and motion to strike signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO MARITIME NATIONAL PARK ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Lauren S. Nishimura of Bingham McCutchen LLP (393.2332) for defendant San Francisco Maritime National Park Association; Megan Jeanne of The Corporate Law Group (650.227.8000) for defendant TWG, LLC; Dominick R. Libonati of Mallette & Libonati (650.347.5400) appearing telephonically for plaintiff Andy Kilday Event Production Services. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained without leave to amend as to 1st, 3rd, and 4th causes of action. The declaratory relief cause of action is superfluous in light of the 2nd cause of action (CCP 1061). The breach of written contract action fails because the contract is not pled verbatim or pled according to its legal intendment and effect. The fraud cause of action is totally conclusory and does not comply with the particularity requirement. Numerous opportunities to amend have been provided and no reasonable probability exists that the defects can be cured by amendment. Overruled as to 2nd cause of action, common count, that may be characterized as quantum meruit, quasi contract, implied in law contract, restitution, or unjust enrichment. Plaintiff alleges the performance of services or work and labor at defendants' request with a promise to pay, and failure to pay. See Witkin, pleading, common count section 566. 10 days leave to answer. One order on demurrer and motion to strike signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
